Overview Healthcoverall Aged Care Education NDIS Veterans Indigenous Affairs
Platform  /  Part II

Social
Services.

Healthcoverall replaces Medicare and private insurance with a single $230B/year program. 1.4 million purpose-built aged care homes. Education, NDIS, Veterans, and Indigenous Affairs rebuilt around outcomes, with delivery measured in service, not in process.

$230B
Healthcoverall, every year
1.4M
Aged care homes built
4 mo
Hospital wait time, was 18
330K
Aged care workforce

Australia's social services have been rebuilt around the assumption that the country can afford to deliver them, because under the new tax system it can. Healthcoverall absorbs the entire previous health spend — federal, state, and out-of-pocket — and adds dental and modernised PBS coverage on top. Aged care has been rescaled by an order of magnitude. The remaining services have been reorganised to deliver outcomes rather than to manage process.

01

Healthcoverall

Medical, dental, and PBS — free at point of service. Wait times slashed. Casinos and retail centres become day-surgery hubs.

Read
02

Aged Care

1.4 million homes. Six times the capacity of the old system. Built in neighbourhoods, not on industrial parcels.

Read
03

Education

Per-learner funding follows the student. Administrative subsidy retired. Detail being finalised with states.

Read
04

NDIS

Transferred to the states with adjusted federal-state transfer formula. The Commonwealth sets standards.

Read
05

Veterans

Historic claims backlog cleared in one payment. Service-related conditions get priority pathways through Healthcoverall.

Read
06

Indigenous Affairs

Outcomes through universal service delivery, with regional weighting. Plan being developed with Indigenous communities.

Read
